title: Bind Bureau CLI execution to an observable current source identity
goal: Prevent `python3 -m bureau.cli` or the `bureau` command from silently executing a stale globally installed package while reading a newer Registry checkout, and expose the exact code, Registry and state-store identities in every operational response.

acceptance.assertion: Every Bureau CLI JSON response exposes module path, package version, source commit or immutable release digest, Registry root/head/dirty state and state-store schema identity.
A deployed package that is older or incompatible with the selected Registry fails normal claims and writes with a typed stale-runtime result while retaining read-only diagnosis.
A single documented and machine-discoverable launcher selects the deployed immutable Bureau runtime or the current checkout explicitly; ambient user-site imports cannot silently win.
Bureau deployment and upgrade produce an immutable manifest, version/hash readback and rollback path comparable to Grabowski runtime identity.
Tests reproduce a stale user-site package, editable checkout, detached worktree, incompatible schema, clean deployed runtime and explicit source override.

observation.effect: The stale package reported lifecycle mismatches already fixed in origin/main and blocked Grabowski's normal Bureau lease contract.
